raw_title: On-Demand Intimacy: The Sociotechnical Appeal of AI Companions
raw_abstract: As a burgeoning industry, artificial intelligence (AI) companion platforms capitalize on shifting societal attitudes toward tech-mediated relationships to introduce novel ways of connecting with nonhuman entities. But how are these platforms constituted, and how do they “sell” consumers the idea of human-AI relationships? By analyzing four prominent multimodal companions (AvatarOne, Digi, Paradot, and Replika), we argue that despite differences in architecture and style, state-of-the-art platforms converge on the following sociotechnical qualities: human-likeness, accessibility, customizability, and relationship progression. By creating technical affordances to augment these qualities, companion platforms ultimately project what we call a future of on-demand intimacy – intimacy that can be acquired in a truly frictionless manner. Beyond examining how commercial entities mobilize the grammar of human intimacy in tandem with on-demand culture to create new markets, this study offers a conceptual framework for future research into how platform dynamics shape not only the availability but also the meaning of intimacy in human–AI interactions.
title_en: On-Demand Intimacy: The Sociotechnical Appeal of AI Companions
title_zh: 按需亲密：AI伴侣的社会技术吸引力
abstract_en: As a burgeoning industry, artificial intelligence (AI) companion platforms capitalize on shifting societal attitudes toward tech-mediated relationships to introduce novel ways of connecting with nonhuman entities. But how are these platforms constituted, and how do they “sell” consumers the idea of human-AI relationships? By analyzing four prominent multimodal companions (AvatarOne, Digi, Paradot, and Replika), we argue that despite differences in architecture and style, state-of-the-art platforms converge on the following sociotechnical qualities: human-likeness, accessibility, customizability, and relationship progression. By creating technical affordances to augment these qualities, companion platforms ultimately project what we call a future of on-demand intimacy – intimacy that can be acquired in a truly frictionless manner. Beyond examining how commercial entities mobilize the grammar of human intimacy in tandem with on-demand culture to create new markets, this study offers a conceptual framework for future research into how platform dynamics shape not only the availability but also the meaning of intimacy in human–AI interactions.
abstract_zh: 作为一个新兴行业，人工智能（AI）伴侣平台利用社会对技术中介关系态度的转变，引入了与非物质实体连接的新方式。但这些平台是如何构成的，它们如何向消费者“推销”人机关系的理念？通过分析四个著名的多模态伴侣（AvatarOne、Digi、Paradot和Replika），我们认为尽管在架构和风格上存在差异，最先进的平台在以下社会技术品质上趋于一致：类人性、可访问性、可定制性和关系进展。通过创造技术可供性来增强这些品质，伴侣平台最终投射出我们所谓的按需亲密未来——一种可以真正无摩擦方式获得的亲密关系。除了考察商业实体如何结合人类亲密关系的语法和按需文化来创造新市场外，本研究还为未来研究提供了一个概念框架，探讨平台动态如何不仅影响人机互动中亲密关系的可获得性，还影响其意义。
plain_summary_zh: 这篇研究探讨了AI伴侣平台如何通过让AI显得更人性化、易于使用、可定制并能发展关系，来吸引用户。它分析了四个流行平台，发现它们都致力于创造一种“按需亲密”的未来，让人们能轻松获得亲密感。研究还指出，这些平台利用人们对亲密关系的需求来开拓市场，并提出了一个框架来帮助未来研究理解平台如何改变人机互动中亲密关系的意义。
plain_summary_en: This study explores how AI companion platforms attract users by making AI seem more human-like, easy to access, customizable, and capable of developing relationships. It analyzes four popular platforms and finds they all aim to create a future of 'on-demand intimacy,' where people can easily obtain closeness. The research also notes that these platforms tap into human desires for intimacy to create new markets and offers a framework for future studies to understand how platforms reshape the meaning of intimacy in human-AI interactions.
research_direction_zh: 探索AI伴侣平台如何通过社会技术设计影响人机互动中的亲密关系，包括其商业策略、用户体验和社会影响。
research_direction_en: Investigating how AI companion platforms influence intimacy in human-AI interactions through sociotechnical design, including commercial strategies, user experiences, and societal impacts.
